gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/347)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/347)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ ما أَصابَكُمْ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فَبِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ أَ رَأَيْتَ مَا أَصَابَ عَلِيّاً وَ أَهْلَ بَيْتِهِ ( عليهم السلام ) مِنْ بَعْدِهِ هُوَ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَ هُمْ أَهْلُ بَيْتِ طَهَارَةٍ مَعْصُومُونَ

فَقَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) كَانَ يَتُوبُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُهُ فِي كُلِّ يَوْمٍ وَ لَيْلَةٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ مِنْ غَيْرِ ذَنْبٍ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَخُصُّ أَوْلِيَاءَهُ بِالْمَصَائِبِ لِيَأْجُرَهُمْ عَلَيْهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ ذَنْبٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, altogether from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali bin Ra’ib who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [42:30] And whatever affliction befalls you, it is on account of what your hands have wrought. What is yourasws view of what hit Aliasws and the Peopleasws of hisasws Household from after himasws, is it due to that theirasws hands had wrought and theyasws are the Pure Peopleasws of the Household, the infallible?’

So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> used to turn to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> and seek His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Forgiveness during every day and night, one hundred times, from without having had sinned. Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Particularised His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Guardians<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> with the difficulties in order to Recompense them<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> from without then<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having had sinned’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father all from ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn Ri’ab who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) about the words of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, ‘Whatever afflictions befall you is because of what your hands have obtained,’ [42:29] do you consider whatever befell Ali and his family (a.s.) after him was because of their deeds and acquisition of their hands despite the fact that they were members of the family of al- Taharah (cleansed by the will of Allah), the infallible people?’ The Imam said, ‘The Messenger of Allah would plead, to return, before Allah seventy times every day and night without any sins. Allah wants his friends, especially, to suffer so He may grant them rewards without sins.’”
